mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales indicates households are sustaining nominal spending, supporting near-term earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot available before decision date was 483066
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp unemployment-rate increase signals an abrupt deterioration in household income and credit quality, causing investors to mark down earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot available before decision date was 4.4
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid federal-funds-rate cut to near zero lowers discount rates and signals policy backstop liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after the crossing occurs.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 1.0 after being above 1.5 within the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot available before decision date was 0.65
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing 60-day S&P 500 returns are not higher than the unconditional 60-day return distribution or if the signal has negative mean return in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates combined with contained CPI inflation reduce equity discount rates without forcing immediate tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1.0 and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation below 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot available before decision date was 257.953
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform periods with only low FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point in average 60-day returns, or if interaction terms are insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and retail sales identify a synchronized production-demand contraction, lowering expected revenues and margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both cross below their 3-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot available before decision date was 103.6635
failure_condition: Reject if the joint downside trigger is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns less than 50% of the time or performs no worse than either single-series trigger alone.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening construction demand and household durable-goods spending, leading to lower cyclical earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 15% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot available before decision date was 1599.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of cases or if HOUST declines have no negative coefficient in walk-forward predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll contraction paired with a rising unemployment rate confirms that labor income is falling rather than merely rotating across sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS crosses below its 3-month moving average while UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot available before decision date was 151786
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor trigger does not produce lower 5-day returns than either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone, or if triggered positive-return frequency exceeds 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving operating leverage and stronger goods-sector earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors update cyclical exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot available before decision date was 103.6635
failure_condition: Reject if higher 1-month INDPRO growth is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is negative.
